stories.title: Update BRAND Node
stories.userStory:
As an API contributor
I want to be able to update a BRAND
So I can add missing information or fix incorrect data
specificationList: A BRAND node is successfully updated when the request contains valid data. The required data structure is specified in parent (epic) ticket MCA-498. -> Status Code `201`
A successful request returns the updated node with all properties that are officially specified.
The `updated_at` timestamp will be updated, the `created_at` timestamp not.
Unknown properties are ignored. They are not processed. They do not produce any info, warning or error messages for the user. -> Status Code `201`
Read-only properties (`id`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) are ignored. They are not processed. They do not produce any info, warning or error messages for the user. -> Status Code `201`
Requests with invalid ID are rejected. This case can happen when there exists no node with that ID or when the node is not from type BRAND. -> Status Code `404`
Requests with invalid data are rejected. Cases for invalid data include: syntax errors, wrong data types, wrong data structure or trying to remove a mandatory field. -> Status Code `400`

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to update a BRAND are accepted when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: Data is valid when its structure is correct and has no syntax errors. Mandatory fields can be overwritten, but not deleted (via null value).

tests.title: Updating a BRAND with valid data
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | name | BMW_2 |
  | full_name | Bayerische Motoren Werke_2 |
  | founded | 1918 |
  | defunct |  |
  | wmi | WBA_2 |
  | hsn | 0005_2 |
  | country_code | DE_2 |
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 201

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests are rejected when there exists no BRAND with the provided ID
acceptanceCriteria.description:
When there exists no node with the provided ID -> rejected.
When there exists a node with the provided ID, but it is not from type BRAND -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to update a non-existing BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
When the user updates the node "BMW"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

acceptanceCriteria.title: Updating a BRAND changes the updated_at timestamp

tests.title: Expecting the updated_at timestamp to change when updating a BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW"
Then both timestamps in the response should be different

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to update a BRAND are rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description: Data is invalid when its structure is incorrect or has syntax errors. Mandatory fields may not not deleted (via null value).

tests.title: Trying to delete a mandatory field while updating a BRAND.
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user tries to update the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | name |       |
Then the request should be rejected with status code 400

tests.title: Trying to update a BRAND with invalid data types
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user tries to update the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | name | 1234 |
  | full_name | 1234 |
  | founded | TEST |
  | defunct | TEST |
  | wmi | 1234 |
  | hsn | 1234 |
  | country_code | 1234 |
Then the request should be rejected with status code 400

acceptanceCriteria.title: The response contains all properties that are officially specified when updating a BRAND
acceptanceCriteria.description: All properties are returned that are specified in the epic MCA-498. When properties are empty they are returned with null values. IDs, timestamp and other meta information might be included too, but is not in the scope of this AC.

tests.title: Expecting all properties to be returned when updating a BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | name | BMW_2 |
  | full_name | Bayerische Motoren Werke_2 |
  | founded | 1918 |
  | defunct |  |
  | wmi | WBA_2 |
  | hsn | 0005_2 |
  | country_code | DE_2 |
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 201
And the response should contain the following properties
  | key | value |
  | name | BMW_2 |
  | full_name | Bayerische Motoren Werke_2 |
  | founded | 1918 |
  | defunct |  |
  | wmi | WBA_2 |
  | hsn | 0005_2 |
  | country_code | DE_2 |

tests.title: Expecting empty properties to be returned as null values when updating a BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | name | BMW_2 |
  | full_name |       |
  | founded |       |
  | defunct |       |
  | wmi |       |
  | hsn |       |
  | country_code |       |
Then the response should contain the following properties
  | key | value |
  | name | BMW_2 |
  | full_name |       |
  | founded |       |
  | defunct |       |
  | wmi |       |
  | hsn |       |
  | country_code |       |

acceptanceCriteria.title: Unknown properties are ignored when updating a BRAND
acceptanceCriteria.description: The user can provide properties that are not specified in the epic MCA-498, but they will be completely ignored. They will not be processed and there will be no info, warning or error that they were skipped.

tests.title: Expecting unknown properties to be ignored when updating a BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key | value |
  | thimbleweed | park |
Then the response should NOT contain the following keys
  | key         |
  | thimbleweed |

acceptanceCriteria.title: Read-only properties cannot be overridden by the user when updating a BRAND
acceptanceCriteria.description: Read-only properties are for example `ID`, `created_at` and `updated_at`. Analog to unknown properties, those fields will be ignored. They will not be processed and there will be no info, warning or error that they were skipped.

tests.title: Expecting read-only properties to be ignored when updating a BRAND
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
When the user updates the node "BMW" with the following data
  | key        | value      |
  | id         | 1234       |
  | created_at | 2025-01-01 |
  | updated_at | 2025-01-01 |
Then the response should contain an ID
And the response should contain the following keys
  | key        |
  | created_at |
  | updated_at |
But the response should NOT contain the ID 1234
And the response should NOT contain the following properties
  | key        | value      |
  | created_at | 2025-01-01 |
  | updated_at | 2025-01-01 |
